items.title: The 6 (single pivot)
items.blurb: The whole machine balances on this player: he needs passing range and elite positional discipline, since a bypassed six leaves the back four exposed. The Busquets archetype.

items.title: The 9
items.blurb: This choice defines the identity: a runner stretches the game vertically, a false nine collapses it centrally, and either choice shapes what the wingers do.

items.title: The 8s
items.blurb: Half-space engines who must combine a high work rate both ways with the craft to receive between the lines.

items.title: The 10
items.blurb: The system exists to feed this player between the lines: he needs press resistance and the final pass, and if he is marked out the shape needs a plan B, usually the wingers in behind. The Özil and Griezmann archetypes.

items.title: The double pivot pairing
items.blurb: Balance is everything: one controller plus one destroyer or runner, such as Alonso and Khedira, or Kimmich and Goretzka. Two of the same profile halves the shape's value.

items.title: The two CMs
items.blurb: Two players must do the work of three: box-to-box engines with discipline, since the shape lives or dies on their compactness.

items.title: The strike partnership
items.blurb: One target or link player plus one runner: their pairing chemistry is the attack, bouncing and running in behind off each other all game.

items.title: The wingbacks
items.blurb: The most demanding role on the pitch: they are the entire width, both ways. Without genuine high-work-rate engines here, this shape should not be picked.

items.title: The central CB
items.blurb: Organiser and spare man: ideally the ball-playing centre back who steps into midfield to create the extra man.

items.title: The double pivot
items.blurb: Two players covering the width of the pitch's engine room: they must be the two fittest, smartest midfielders in the squad.

items.title: The wide forwards
items.blurb: Half-space assassins playing off the nine, with wingbacks supplying width outside them.

items.title: The outlet striker
items.blurb: The most important defender on the team plays up front: if he cannot hold the ball or win his sprint, the block never breathes.

items.title: The central CB of the five
items.blurb: Commands the box, wins everything in the air, and organises the line vocally.
